texto: Like almost all of Beefheart's recorded work, [Trout Mask Replica] was not even "ahead" of its time in 1969. Then and now, it stands outside time, trends, fads, hypes, the rise and fall of whole genres eclectic as walking Christmas trees, constituting a genre unto itself: truly, a musical Monolith if ever there was one.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lester Bangs: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Like almost all of Beefheart's recorded work, [Trout Mask Replica] was not even "ahead" of its ti…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, beatles, beefheart pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de natal e termino.

texto: They're events you remember all your life, like your first real orgasm. And the whole purpose of the absurd, mechanically persistent involvement with recorded music is the pursuit of that priceless moment. So it's not exactly that records might unhinge the mind, but rather that if anything is going to drive you up the wall it might as well be a record.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lester Bangs: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. texto: if the main reason we listen to music in the first place is to hear passion expressed- as i've believed all my life-then what good is this music going to prove to be? what does that say about us? what are we confirming in ourselves by doting on art that is emotionally neutral? and, simultaneously, what in ourselves might we be destroying or at least keeping down?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Lester Bangs que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “if the main reason we listen to music in the first place is to hear passion expressed- as i've be…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, music, ourselves, com foco em reflexao.

texto: Every great work of art has two faces, one toward its own time and one toward the future, toward eternity.

texto: It's not enough just to do those things anymore; what you must do instead if you want success on any large scale is either figure out a way of getting yourself associated in the audience's mind with their pieties and their sense of 'community,' i.e. ram it home that you're one of THEM; or, alternately, deck and bake yourself into an image configuration so blatant or outrageous that you become a culture myth.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Lester Bangs sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. A afirmação “It's not enough just to do those things anymore; what you must do instead if you want success on …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sucesso, music, run-on-sentences, útil em momentos de run-on-sentences.

texto: I'll probably never produce a masterpiece, but so what? I feel I have a Sound aborning, which is my own, and that Sound if erratic is still my greatest pride, because I would rather write like a dancer shaking my ass to boogaloo inside my head, and perhaps reach only readers who like to use books to shake their asses, than to be or write for the man cloistered in a closet somewhere reading Aeschylus while this stupefying world careens crazily past his waxy windows toward its last raving sooty feedback pirouette.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lester Bangs: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I'll probably never produce a masterpiece, but so what? I feel I have a Sound aborning, which is …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, writing, sound pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
